title: Resolve transitive dependency version conflicts
description: Read the dependency tree, understand the build tool's conflict-resolution rule, and pin versions via a BOM or constraints instead of guessing.
body: Use when a NoSuchMethodError, NoClassDefFoundError, or duplicate class appears at runtime. Do not add random exclusions until it compiles or assume the highest version wins (Maven picks nearest-wins, Gradle picks highest). Actions: run `mvn dependency:tree -Dverbose` or `gradle dependencies` to find the conflicting versions and their paths, align them with a BOM (dependencyManagement / platform()) or an explicit constraint, and remove only the duplicate, not the API you need. Validation signals include a single resolved version per artifact in the tree and the runtime error disappearing. Risk boundary: a forced downgrade can reintroduce a security fix's absence; verify the chosen version against advisories and escalate rather than silently pinning an old release.
